campaign.title: Real-Time Alerts for Wildfire Safety
campaign.description:
Wildfires are becoming more frequent and more destructive every year. In California alone, millions of people live in fire-prone areas, and the difference between safety and disaster can come down to minutes — sometimes seconds — of warning.

Watch Duty is a nonprofit organization that provides free, real-time wildfire alerts to communities across the Western United States. Unlike government alert systems that can be slow or overly broad, Watch Duty uses a network of trained, real firefighters and first responders who monitor fire activity around the clock and push hyper-local notifications directly to your phone.

Since launching, Watch Duty has delivered over 50 million alerts and helped countless families evacuate safely. Their reporting is trusted by fire departments, emergency managers, and everyday people who depend on accurate, timely information when it matters most.

Why We're Fundraising

Watch Duty runs entirely on donations — no ads, no paywalls, no data selling. Every dollar goes toward keeping the platform running: server infrastructure, firefighter stipends for overnight monitoring shifts, and expanding coverage to new at-risk regions.

Our goal is to raise $3,000 to directly support Watch Duty's operations during the upcoming fire season. Here's how your donation helps:

  • $25 — Covers one hour of a trained monitor's shift
  • $100 — Funds server costs for a day of real-time alerting
  • $500 — Sponsors monitoring expansion into a new county

This Is Personal

I started this fundraiser after my own neighborhood in Los Altos received a Watch Duty alert about a brush fire just two miles away. That alert gave my family the time we needed to prepare — pack essentials, check on elderly neighbors, and have an evacuation plan ready. The fire was contained, but the peace of mind was invaluable.

Please consider donating and sharing this campaign. Every contribution, no matter the size, brings us closer to a future where no one is caught off guard by a wildfire. Together, we can keep our communities safe.

Watch Duty mobile app showing real-time fire perimeter map with alert notifications
A Watch Duty volunteer firefighter monitoring multiple screens in a dispatch center
Neighbors helping each other evacuate during a wildfire warning in a California suburb

organizer.bio: Software engineer and community advocate passionate about using technology to keep people safe. Longtime volunteer with local emergency preparedness groups in the Bay Area.

beneficiary.name: Watch Duty
beneficiary.description: Watch Duty is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing free, real-time wildfire alerts powered by trained firefighters and first responders. Donations are tax-deductible.

donations.message: Watch Duty saved our family during the 2025 fires. We owe them everything. Please keep doing this incredible work.
Great cause, Janahan. Sharing with my whole neighborhood group.
Every second counts in a wildfire. Thank you for supporting the people who give us those seconds.
My parents live in Napa. Watch Duty is the only app I trust to keep them informed. Donating gladly.
As a first responder myself, I know how critical real-time info is. Watch Duty does it right.
Janahan told me about this at work — happy to chip in. Let's hit that goal!
Matching my company's donation. Wildfire preparedness matters more than ever with climate change accelerating.

comments.message: We evacuated with 20 minutes to spare last October because of a Watch Duty alert. Without it, we would have been caught completely off guard. This nonprofit deserves every penny.
Just shared this with our Nextdoor group. Everyone in fire country should know about Watch Duty. Great fundraiser, Janahan!
Thank you all so much for the support! We're over 70% of the way to our goal. Every share helps just as much as every dollar.
The fact that real firefighters are the ones monitoring and sending alerts is what makes Watch Duty different from everything else out there. It's not an algorithm — it's human expertise.
I work in emergency services and I can confirm: Watch Duty's alerts are often faster and more accurate than official channels. They're filling a gap that desperately needed filling.
Just installed the app after donating. The level of detail on fire perimeters is incredible. Why isn't this more well-known?
My company matched my donation! If your employer has a matching program, check if Watch Duty qualifies — they're a registered 501(c)(3).
Small donation but sending love from Oregon. We deal with fire season up here too and I wish Watch Duty covered our area. Hopefully this helps them expand.

community.name: Watch Duty
community.description:
Watch Duty is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing free, real-time wildfire alerts to communities across the Western United States. Our service is powered by a network of trained, active and retired firefighters, dispatchers, and first responders who monitor fire activity around the clock.

Since launching, we've delivered over 50 million alerts and helped countless families evacuate safely. Our reporting is trusted by fire departments, emergency managers, and everyday people who depend on accurate, timely information when it matters most.

Our Mission

We believe everyone deserves access to life-saving wildfire information — regardless of where they live or what they can afford. Watch Duty will always be free, ad-free, and community-supported.

How We Work

  • Real firefighters — not algorithms — monitor fire activity 24/7
  • Hyper-local alerts pushed directly to your phone in seconds
  • Live fire maps with perimeter data updated in real time
  • Community reporting lets neighbors help neighbors stay informed

Every dollar raised through our community goes directly toward keeping monitors staffed, servers running, and coverage expanding to new at-risk regions.
community.tagline: Stay Informed. Stay Safe. Stay Ready.

campaigns.title: Rural Fire Alert Coverage for Mendocino County
